evidence: Instead of just sharing resumes, which is the common practice we see in tech recruiters, we want to go up the value chain and potentially leverage career span if it can integrate into the workflow.

If you saw recently Harvey AI Anthropic OpenAI are building teams in India. And so we're building teams in that structure. Not you're contracting a vendor that is managing the resources. It's actually your embedded team.
Our company is building a really strong pool of engineering talent and teams across India. To date we've built a few teams for seed to series C startups.
On the other side it's building AI engineering pods specific to your company's workflows or technology you're looking to build out. That will be the more scalable business.

A customer gets a lot more visibility than just a Resume and a LinkedIn - they get this more holistic picture of what a candidate is like, which is important when you're hiring a team or a person abroad.

People hiring in America have expectations that you can talk about yourself like this. Whether or not it's necessary for you to do your job is up for debate. But it's unfortunately necessary in the US job market.
